cards.front: Which Python data type is used to represent whole numbers like -5 or 42?
What data type are numbers with a decimal point, such as 3.14?
In Python, what characters must always surround a string value?
What is the specific name for a string with no characters, written as ''?
Which math operator is used for exponentiation (e.g., $2^3$)?
What is the result of the integer division (floor division) operator //?
cards.back: The quotient rounded down to the nearest whole number.

cards.front: Which operator returns the remainder of a division?
What term describes a combination of values and operators that evaluates to a single value?
Which function is used to display text or values on the screen?
Which function allows a program to receive text input from the keyboard?
What does the len() function return when passed a string?
cards.back: The number of characters in the string as an integer.

cards.front: How can you convert the integer 10 into the string '10'?
Which function converts a value into a floating-point number?
Term: Interactive Shell (REPL)
cards.back: Definition: A program that executes Python instructions one at a time and shows results immediately.

cards.front: In Python, the assignment operator is the _____ sign.
What occurs to the old value of a variable when a new value is assigned to it?
cards.back: The old value is forgotten or overwritten.

cards.front: Which function evaluates the data type of a specific value?
Which function returns the absolute value of a number (e.g., converting -25 to 25)?
What is the Python comparison operator for 'not equal to'?
cards.back: The exclamation point and equal sign ($!=$).

cards.front: Which Boolean operator evaluates to True only if both sub-expressions are True?
Which Boolean operator evaluates to True if at least one sub-expression is True?
The _____ operator evaluates to the opposite Boolean value of the expression it precedes.
How does Python determine which lines of code belong to the same block?
Which flow control statement executes a block if a condition is True, and skips to an optional block if it is False?
Which statement provides a way to check multiple mutually exclusive conditions after an initial 'if'?
Which loop type continues to execute its block as long as a certain condition remains True?
What statement causes the program execution to immediately exit a loop?
Which statement causes the program to jump back to the start of the loop and re-evaluate the condition?
Which function is commonly used with a 'for' loop to iterate a specific number of times?
Term: Module
cards.back: Definition: A library of Python code containing functions that can be imported into a program.

cards.front: Which keyword is used to create and define a new function?
What value is automatically returned by a function that does not have a return statement?
How do you specify a different separator between items in a print() function call?
cards.back: By passing a string to the 'sep' keyword argument.

cards.front: What data type is an ordered collection of values typically written inside square brackets []?
What is the index of the very first item in a Python list?
What integer index is used to access the last item in a list regardless of its length?
What is the process of creating a new list from a range of indices in an existing list?
Term: Tuple
cards.back: Definition: An immutable sequence of values, similar to a list but written with parentheses ().

cards.front: What is the primary difference between a list and a tuple?
cards.back: Lists are mutable (changeable) while tuples are immutable.

cards.front: Which data type uses key-value pairs to organize data?
Which character sequence represents a newline inside a string?
Which character sequence represents a tab inside a string?
How can you include a literal backslash character in a Python string?
Term: Regular Expression (Regex)
cards.back: Definition: A sequence of characters that forms a search pattern for text matching.

cards.front: Which Python module is used for working with regular expressions?
Term: Web Scraping
cards.back: Definition: Using a program to automatically download and process content from the internet.

cards.front: Which third-party module is used to download web pages and files from a URL?
What method on a requests response object ensures that a download was successful by raising an exception on error?
Which library is used to parse and extract information from HTML documents?
What method in Beautiful Soup retrieves elements matching a CSS selector string?
Which library allows Python to control a web browser to simulate mouse clicks and form filling?
What is 'headless mode' in browser automation?
cards.back: Running a browser simulation without an visible window on the screen.

cards.front: Which module allows Python to read and modify .xlsx Excel files?
In openpyxl, which function is used to open an existing spreadsheet file?
Which attribute of an openpyxl Workbook object provides the titles of all sheets in the file?
How do you access the value of cell A1 from a worksheet object named 'sheet' in openpyxl?
Which openpyxl sheet method is used to combine multiple cells into one?
Which module provides a simple interface for interacting with Google Sheets?
Which module is used to simulate keyboard presses and mouse movements to automate GUI tasks?
What is the primary risk of using PyAutoGUI for automation?
cards.back: It is 'blind' to the screen and can fail if windows move or resolution changes.

cards.front: Which PyAutoGUI method is used to simulate typing a string of text?
What does a 'fail-safe' do in PyAutoGUI?
cards.back: It stops the program if the mouse is moved to a corner of the screen.

cards.front: Which library is used to programmatically read and edit Microsoft Word (.docx) files?
What does 'CSV' stand for in the context of data files?
What two data formats are most commonly used by online APIs to return responses?
Which function in the json module converts a JSON-formatted string into a Python dictionary?
Which module is used to handle dates and times in Python?
What does the time.sleep() function do?
cards.back: It pauses the program execution for a specified number of seconds.

cards.front: Which module allows Python to send and receive emails?
Term: Web Crawler
cards.back: Definition: A program that automatically follows links on web pages to download or index data.

cards.front: What is the keyboard shortcut to open Developer Tools in most web browsers?
Which CSS selector would you use to find an element with the 'id' attribute 'main'?
Which CSS selector would you use to find all elements with the class 'highlight'?
What is the purpose of the 'freeze pane' feature in Excel?
cards.back: To keep specific rows or columns visible while scrolling through the rest of the sheet.

cards.front: In programming, what is a 'bug'?
cards.back: An error in the code that causes it to behave unexpectedly.

cards.front: What term describes the process of finding and fixing errors in code?
Which keyword is used to exit a function and send a value back to the caller?
What is a 'global variable'?
cards.back: A variable defined outside of any function that can be accessed by any part of the program.

cards.front: What happens when you pass a negative number to the range() function, such as range(5, -1, -1)?
cards.back: It creates a sequence that counts down from the first number to the second.

cards.front: What is the name of the author of 'Automate the Boring Stuff with Python'?
